Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Penn Pipe And Supply
Off Calendar Jan-24-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for application for entry of default judgment against Defendant Penn Pipe & Supply.

Dept:505; Judge: Maria Evangelista; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:36:52 a.m., pausing at 9:40:59 a.m., reconvening at 9:42:29 a.m. and concluding at 9:57:59 a.m.(D505)

Kimberly Chu, Esq. appearing for and on behalf of Plaintiff Jacob De Boer via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Penn Pipe and Supply not appearing.

Plaintiff's 1st amended complaint for Asbestos filed December 7, 2016
Proof of personal service as to Defendant Penn Pipe & Supply on December 30, 2016
Default entered as to Defendant Penn Pipe & Supply on November 22, 2023

Counsel to summarize the causes of action and to provide proof.
Court takes a brief recess at 9:40:59 a.m.
Proceedings reconvene at 9:42:29 a.m.
Counsel rectified connectivity issues. Counsel continues with summarizing and providing proof to the Court.
Court inquires as to the cause of death of Plaintiff. Counsel clarifies.
Counsel to provide proof as to non-economic damages.

Plaintiff submits the following exhibits for review of the Court:
Exhibit A: Medical Report
Exhibit B: Proof of Default, service of summons and statement of damages
Exhibit C: Operative Complaint
Exhibit D: Declaration of Brenda De Boer
Exhibit E: Declaration of James P. Nevin, Esq.
Exhibit F: Verified work history of Plaintiff

After reviewing the declarations, considering all the evidence, the Court finds that the Plaintiff has met their prima facie case and rules in favor of Plaintiff Jacob De Boer and against Defendant as follows:

Plaintiff is to recover from Defendant:
Damages $1,000,000.00

Proposed Judgment signed and executed this day.
